2026-04-21T03:31:38.6851925Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6852515Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6852940Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6853304Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6853621Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6853861Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6854016Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T03:31:38.6854190Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-21T03:31:38.6855013Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-21T03:31:38.6855764Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The files are in the `/doc` folder rather than `/eng`, but the nature of this is still engineering tooling documentation. Since "Central-EngSys" is excluded from automatic triage despite being the most fitting category, I should apply "needs-triage" instead and let the team determine the proper classification. the issue is about documentation for a GeneratorMigration tooling system, which doesn't fit neatly into the standard categories like Client, Service, Management, or Provisioning. If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-21T03:33:58.8624729Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T03:32:48.686358544Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58420,\"title\":\"[GeneratorMigration] Docs: Update Library_Inventory_Guide.md to document new SCM column, Provisioning section, and scan progress\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `doc/GeneratorMigration`\\n**Service directory:** `doc/GeneratorMigration/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58372 (commit f288cfe) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58372 updated `Library_Inventory.ps1` to add:\\n1. A **`Using SCM`** column in the Data Plane Libraries (DPG) migrated-to-new-emitter table — marks libraries using `@typespec/http-client-csharp` (System.ClientModel) with ✅\\n2. A new **Provisioning Libraries** section in the generated report, with columns for `Mgmt Peer Library` and `Generator` (Reflection / TypeSpec / None), plus a migration status line\\n3. New **scan progress reporting** (`Write-Progress` + `Write-Host` messages) emitted while the script scans all service directories\\n\\nHowever, the companion document `Library_Inventory_Guide.md` was **not updated** and still describes the old output format.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `Library_Inventory_Guide.md` \\u0026#34;Understanding the Results\\u0026#34; section describes only two columns (`Service | Library | New Emitter`) for the DPG migrated table, but the output now has four columns (`Service | Library | New Emitter | Using SCM`)\\n- The `Using SCM` column is not explained anywhere in the guide (what it means, which emitter package triggers it, how to interpret ✅ vs blank)\\n- The guide does not mention the **Provisioning Libraries** section at all — neither the table structure (`Service | Library | Mgmt Peer Library | Generator`), the generator values (`Reflection`, `TypeSpec`, `None`), nor the migration status line\\n- The guide does not mention the scan progress output printed to the console while the script runs\\n- The summary statistics section in the guide does not reflect the new Provisioning breakdown (`Reflection-based generator`, `TypeSpec-based generator`, `No generator`)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `doc/GeneratorMigration/Library_Inventory_Guide.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — update the \\u0026#34;Understanding the Results\\u0026#34; section\\n\\n#### 1a. Update the Data Plane Migrated table description\\n\\nLocate and replace the block that describes the DPG migrated table columns:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - **Data Plane Libraries (DPG) - Migrated to New Emitter**: Client APIs using TypeSpec\\n     - Columns: Service, Library, New Emitter\\n     - Migration status shows: migrated / total TypeSpec libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;16 / 32 (50%)\\u0026#34;)\\n     - **Only includes libraries with tsp-location.yaml file**\\n     - Shows ✅ for libraries using new emitters, blank for old TypeSpec\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - **Data Plane Libraries (DPG) - Migrated to New Emitter**: Client APIs using TypeSpec\\n     - Columns: Service, Library, New Emitter, Using SCM\\n     - Migration status shows: migrated / total TypeSpec libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;16 / 32 (50%)\\u0026#34;)\\n     - **Only includes libraries with tsp-location.yaml file**\\n     - Shows ✅ in **New Emitter** for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters; blank for old TypeSpec\\n     - Shows ✅ in **Using SCM** for libraries whose `tsp-location.yaml` references the `@typespec/http-client-csharp` emitter — these libraries are built on `System.ClientModel` rather than `Azure.Core`; blank otherwise\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add a Provisioning Libraries entry to the results section\\n\\nAfter the \\u0026#34;5. Libraries with no generator\\u0026#34; bullet (currently the last bullet), add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n6. **Provisioning Libraries**: Infrastructure-as-code libraries built on the `Azure.Provisioning.*` pattern\\n   - Columns: Service, Library, Mgmt Peer Library, Generator\\n   - **Mgmt Peer Library**: The management-plane (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`) package that the provisioning library wraps. Libraries whose peer has been migrated to the new emitter display ✅ next to the peer name\\n   - **Generator** values:\\n     - `TypeSpec ✅` — library is generated from TypeSpec using the `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` emitter\\n     - `Reflection` — library uses the legacy reflection-based provisioning generator\\n     - `None` — library has no code generator\\n   - Migration status shows: TypeSpec-based count / total provisioning libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;3 / 40 migrated to TypeSpec-based generator\\u0026#34;)\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the legend / column-description block near the bottom\\n\\nLocate the existing legend block:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe migration tables use ✅ emoji to indicate the generator type:\\n- **New Emitter** column: ✅ for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, or `@typespec/http-client-csharp`)\\n- **No checkmark**: Libraries using old TypeSpec (TSP-Old) with tsp-location.yaml but not yet migrated to new emitter\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe migration tables use ✅ emoji to indicate status:\\n- **New Emitter** column: ✅ for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, or `@typespec/http-client-csharp`); blank for old TypeSpec (TSP-Old)\\n- **Using SCM** column (Data Plane table only): ✅ for libraries built on `System.ClientModel` (`@typespec/http-client-csharp` emitter); blank for libraries built on `Azure.Core`\\n- **Generator** column (Provisioning table): `TypeSpec ✅`, `Reflection`, or `None` indicating the generator used for each provisioning library\\n- **Mgmt Peer Library** column (Provisioning table): the corresponding `Azure.ResourceManager.*` package; ✅ next to the name means that peer has been migrated to the new TypeSpec emitter\\n```\\n\\n#### 1d. Add a note about scan progress output\\n\\nAfter the existing \\u0026#34;Usage\\u0026#34; step 2 block (which lists what the script does), add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - Prints scan progress to the console in 10% increments (e.g., `Scan progress: 30% (45/150 services)`) and uses PowerShell\\u0026#39;s `Write-Progress` for a progress bar in supporting terminals\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo code snippets are needed — this is a pure prose documentation update.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\n`Library_Inventory_Guide.md` is an internal tooling guide, not a published SDK README. Standard SDK README sections do not apply here. Verify only that:\\n- All four new items above are present and accurate\\n- The guide still reads coherently from top to bottom after the edits\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. Run the script to regenerate the inventory and confirm its output matches the guide\\u0026#39;s description:\\n   ```powershell\\n   pwsh doc/GeneratorMigration/Library_Inventory.ps1\\n   ```\\n2. Visually diff `doc/GeneratorMigration/Library_Inventory.md` against the descriptions in the updated guide to confirm accuracy\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-21T03:33:58.8777455Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T03:33:24.307260188Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58420,\"title\":\"[GeneratorMigration] Docs: Update Library_Inventory_Guide.md to document new SCM column, Provisioning section, and scan progress\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `doc/GeneratorMigration`\\n**Service directory:** `doc/GeneratorMigration/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58372 (commit f288cfe) by `@ArthurMa1978`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58372 updated `Library_Inventory.ps1` to add:\\n1. A **`Using SCM`** column in the Data Plane Libraries (DPG) migrated-to-new-emitter table — marks libraries using `@typespec/http-client-csharp` (System.ClientModel) with ✅\\n2. A new **Provisioning Libraries** section in the generated report, with columns for `Mgmt Peer Library` and `Generator` (Reflection / TypeSpec / None), plus a migration status line\\n3. New **scan progress reporting** (`Write-Progress` + `Write-Host` messages) emitted while the script scans all service directories\\n\\nHowever, the companion document `Library_Inventory_Guide.md` was **not updated** and still describes the old output format.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `Library_Inventory_Guide.md` \\u0026#34;Understanding the Results\\u0026#34; section describes only two columns (`Service | Library | New Emitter`) for the DPG migrated table, but the output now has four columns (`Service | Library | New Emitter | Using SCM`)\\n- The `Using SCM` column is not explained anywhere in the guide (what it means, which emitter package triggers it, how to interpret ✅ vs blank)\\n- The guide does not mention the **Provisioning Libraries** section at all — neither the table structure (`Service | Library | Mgmt Peer Library | Generator`), the generator values (`Reflection`, `TypeSpec`, `None`), nor the migration status line\\n- The guide does not mention the scan progress output printed to the console while the script runs\\n- The summary statistics section in the guide does not reflect the new Provisioning breakdown (`Reflection-based generator`, `TypeSpec-based generator`, `No generator`)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `doc/GeneratorMigration/Library_Inventory_Guide.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — update the \\u0026#34;Understanding the Results\\u0026#34; section\\n\\n#### 1a. Update the Data Plane Migrated table description\\n\\nLocate and replace the block that describes the DPG migrated table columns:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - **Data Plane Libraries (DPG) - Migrated to New Emitter**: Client APIs using TypeSpec\\n     - Columns: Service, Library, New Emitter\\n     - Migration status shows: migrated / total TypeSpec libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;16 / 32 (50%)\\u0026#34;)\\n     - **Only includes libraries with tsp-location.yaml file**\\n     - Shows ✅ for libraries using new emitters, blank for old TypeSpec\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - **Data Plane Libraries (DPG) - Migrated to New Emitter**: Client APIs using TypeSpec\\n     - Columns: Service, Library, New Emitter, Using SCM\\n     - Migration status shows: migrated / total TypeSpec libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;16 / 32 (50%)\\u0026#34;)\\n     - **Only includes libraries with tsp-location.yaml file**\\n     - Shows ✅ in **New Emitter** for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters; blank for old TypeSpec\\n     - Shows ✅ in **Using SCM** for libraries whose `tsp-location.yaml` references the `@typespec/http-client-csharp` emitter — these libraries are built on `System.ClientModel` rather than `Azure.Core`; blank otherwise\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Add a Provisioning Libraries entry to the results section\\n\\nAfter the \\u0026#34;5. Libraries with no generator\\u0026#34; bullet (currently the last bullet), add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n6. **Provisioning Libraries**: Infrastructure-as-code libraries built on the `Azure.Provisioning.*` pattern\\n   - Columns: Service, Library, Mgmt Peer Library, Generator\\n   - **Mgmt Peer Library**: The management-plane (`Azure.ResourceManager.*`) package that the provisioning library wraps. Libraries whose peer has been migrated to the new emitter display ✅ next to the peer name\\n   - **Generator** values:\\n     - `TypeSpec ✅` — library is generated from TypeSpec using the `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` emitter\\n     - `Reflection` — library uses the legacy reflection-based provisioning generator\\n     - `None` — library has no code generator\\n   - Migration status shows: TypeSpec-based count / total provisioning libraries (e.g., \\u0026#34;3 / 40 migrated to TypeSpec-based generator\\u0026#34;)\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the legend / column-description block near the bottom\\n\\nLocate the existing legend block:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe migration tables use ✅ emoji to indicate the generator type:\\n- **New Emitter** column: ✅ for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, or `@typespec/http-client-csharp`)\\n- **No checkmark**: Libraries using old TypeSpec (TSP-Old) with tsp-location.yaml but not yet migrated to new emitter\\n```\\n\\nReplace with:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThe migration tables use ✅ emoji to indicate status:\\n- **New Emitter** column: ✅ for libraries using new TypeSpec emitters (`@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp`, `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`, or `@typespec/http-client-csharp`); blank for old TypeSpec (TSP-Old)\\n- **Using SCM** column (Data Plane table only): ✅ for libraries built on `System.ClientModel` (`@typespec/http-client-csharp` emitter); blank for libraries built on `Azure.Core`\\n- **Generator** column (Provisioning table): `TypeSpec ✅`, `Reflection`, or `None` indicating the generator used for each provisioning library\\n- **Mgmt Peer Library** column (Provisioning table): the corresponding `Azure.ResourceManager.*` package; ✅ next to the name means that peer has been migrated to the new TypeSpec emitter\\n```\\n\\n#### 1d. Add a note about scan progress output\\n\\nAfter the existing \\u0026#34;Usage\\u0026#34; step 2 block (which lists what the script does), add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n   - Prints scan progress to the console in 10% increments (e.g., `Scan progress: 30% (45/150 services)`) and uses PowerShell\\u0026#39;s `Write-Progress` for a progress bar in supporting terminals\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo code snippets are needed — this is a pure prose documentation update.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\n`Library_Inventory_Guide.md` is an internal tooling guide, not a published SDK README. Standard SDK README sections do not apply here. Verify only that:\\n- All four new items above are present and accurate\\n- The guide still reads coherently from top to bottom after the edits\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1.
